---
title: |
  <span style="font-size: 1.0em;">Survival Trees and Random Survival Forests</span>
subtitle: |
  <div style="font-size: 0.8em; line-height: 1.6; text-align: center;">
  Jakob Haas <br>
  **Supervisor:** Dr. Andreas Bender <br>
  </div>
date: "2026-06-05"
format:
  revealjs:
    css: customstyle.css
    slide-number: c
    center: false
editor: source
---

```{r setup, include=FALSE}
options(repos = c(CRAN = "https://cran.rstudio.com"))
source("setup.R")
```

## Motivation




## Tables of Content

1.  Introdution

2.  From CART to Survival Tree

3.  From Survival Tree to Random Survival Forest

4.  Evaluation

5.  Comparison with Cox Regression

6.  Conclusion



## CART: Construction

:::: {.columns}

::: {.column width = "45%"}
![](results/iris_tree.png){width=100%}
:::

::: {.column width = "55%"}

**How CART works**

1.  Start with a root node that contains all the data

2.  Search for the feature $x_p$ and split that minimizes the empirical risk in the child nodes

3.  Repeat recursively for each node

4.  Stop when a stopping criterion is reached
:::

::::

## CART: Prediction



:::: {.columns}

::: {.column width = "45%"}
![](results/iris_tree.png){width=100%}
:::


::: {.column width = "55%"}
**New Observation**
<div class="small-table">
```{r}
knitr::kable(data.frame(
  Feature = c("Sepal.Length", "Sepal.Width", "Petal.Length", "Petal.Width"),
  Value = c(5.1, 3.5, 2.8, 0.2)
))
```
</div>
:::
::::

$\Rightarrow$ Prediction $$
